text: robotic assisted repair of recurrent right inguinal hernia with mesh by Dr. Andrew Iraniha. This is a fifty-two year old male with history of right inguinal hernia repair ten years ago who presented with a painful right groin bulge and found to have recurrent right inguinal hernia. This video demonstrates the robotic assisted repair of recurrent right inguinal hernia with mesh and its challenges due to significant scarring secondary to the previous hernia repair. This video is also a testimony for valuable role of the robotic surgery in performing recurrent hernia repair.

The surgeon creates a transverse incision in the peritoneum from the anterior superior iliac spine towards the medial umbilical ligament. The preperitoneal space is entered, and a preperitoneal flap is created. Dissection continues medially towards the hernia defect, revealing significant scarring from the previous repair. Sutures from the prior surgery are visible. Using risted robotic instruments, the surgeon performs precise dissection despite the scarring. A three-dimensional mesh is placed into the pre-peritoneal space to cover the defect, ensuring appropriate coverage to prevent future recurrence.

The surgeon secures the mesh medially to Cooper's ligaments using 2-0 ethibond sutures in a simple interrupted fashion. The remaining mesh is affixed to the abdominal wall with surgical glue. The peritoneal defect is closed primarily with absorbable sutures in a simple running fashion.
The surgeon includes the hernia sac and preperitoneal fat in the closure to reinforce the peritoneal flap, completing the repair of the recurrent right inguinal hernia.
This video is a testimony for valuable role of robotic platform in performing recurrent hernia repair.
